In his closing remarks, Mr. O'Vine looked back fondly on his time thus far in the department: "I've been practicing animal husbandry for thirty-seven years, and they only convicted me once!"

### Reactions

Speaking to the *Revue* prior to the event, SCOFF president Ewegene Woolsey expressed his concerns:

"We \[SCOFF\] think that the event will be unimaginable baa-d for the cause of animal rights. The animal husbandry department is trying to pull the wool over our eyes: they say that it will be too expensive to host the event anywhere other than the sheep pasture and then turn around and absolutely fleece us on the tuition. How can any baaptised Catholic think that this is how Jesus would treat his flock! This is so bleatantly wrong! Shear insanity! The university's sheepish inability to condemn this event is an indictment of the President and the Board of Directors. We will stand no longer for their wooly appeals to academic freedom!"

A SPECTRUM spokesperson told the *Revue*, "We like doing unspeakable things to sheep too! There's solidarity and allyship to be found at Notre Dame if you just look hard enough."

The Animal Husbandry Department called the event a "smashing, nay, bone-crunching success."

While they have not yet announced a vigil service for those who were killed during the talk, on Tuesday they laid out plans to dress foxes as henhouse guards for their next event.

title: Rime of the Ancient Senator
content:
It is ancient senator,  
One on a spending spree.  
"By thy sleepy gaze and rambling speech  
Now wherefore tax thou me?  

"The stack of bills is piling high;  
My paycheck stretches thin;  
I have four kids, and yet am I  
To take this on the chin?"  

The ancient nose leans to his hair,  
"You use Old Spice" quoth Joe.  
"Get off! Sniff not, you weird old loon!  
And give me back my dough!"  

Joe holds him with a "C'mon, man!"  
The taxpayer stands still,  
And listens to the rantings of  
The old man from The Hill.  

The taxpayer sat down in debt,  
Next to imperator;  
And thus spake on that ancient man,  
The now ex-senator.  

"The race was won, the country ours,  
Merrily did we wend  
Into the helm, behind the desk,  
And then proceed to spend.  

"The party shifted to the left,  
We stood without a chance,  
Unless we bent, unless we spent,  
Your money in advance.  

"Higher and higher every day,  
Our debt grew on The Hill —"  
The taxpayer here beat his breast,  
For he got another bill.  

The grocery store now has no food,  
To feed his newborn son.  
The price of gas has gone up fast,  
Our pres'dent's mind is done.  

The taxpayer, he beat his breast,  
For there's nothing he can do,  
And thus spake on that ancient man,  
Who through our billions blew.  

"And then the POLLING came, and it  
Was negative, and strong:  
It struck with thoughts of a Red Wave,  
And pulled the Left along.  

"With dipping polls and higher gas,  
Without a chance in sight,  
Conservatives seemed destined to  
Retake and win the fight.  

"We had to act, had to engage,  
The base from which we pull!  
We had to act, had to enrich,  
And make their coffers full!  

"And so we planned a dev'ous thing,  
A payout to our base.  
More blatant acts have not been done  
Upon this planet's face.  

"You see, therefore, we have to pay  
For Emily's degree.  
If she can't backwards basket-weave,  
We'll lose the whole country!  

"You see, therefore, we have to pay  
Off Jordan's art degree!  
Without him out there working hard,  
We'll lack slam poetry!  

"You see, therefore, we must now act  
And give McKenzie cash  
For if we don't we're sure to face  
Her internet backlash!"  

"I think this scheme is all to buy  
Yourselves votes in the Fall!"  
But if Joe Biden heard his quip,  
He made no sign at all.  

"You see, therefore, we have to pay  
For Chad's small student debt!  
He drank all night, and dropped out fast  
He shouldn't have to fret!  

"You see therefore, we have to pay,  
And by we I mean you  
As when to children parents say  
'"We" ought nap for a few.'  

"So don't be sad, although you worked  
So hard to pay for school.  
Or even worse, you never went,  
And so this all seems cruel.  

"You know that we do what is best,  
(Or just what's best for us).  
And so you must pay off their loans  
And do so with no fuss."  

The Senator, whose mind has gone,  
Whose words do not make sense,  
Stood up and walked back to his home,  
And left a debt immense.  

The taxpayer himself was left  
And is of sense forlorn.  
A sadder and a poorer man,  
He rose the morrow morn.
